key_insight: Integrating differentiable finite element analysis into stellarator coil optimization lets engineers co-design coil geometry and support clamps to improve both magnetic performance and structural safety.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the trial-and-error burden of placing supports by hand, enabling faster, more manufacturable fusion magnet designs with lower coil stress.
method: Built an open-source tool, coil-fem, that adds differentiable structural mechanics to the coil optimization loop.
Jointly optimized coil shape and support clamp locations with a stress penalty term.
Balanced magnetic field error against structural rigidity in one integrated workflow.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a resulting coil set with 2.4× lower RMS von Mises stress and similar field error versus an unoptimized baseline.
watch_item: Results are shown on a design case from the abstract, so practical gains may depend on model fidelity, material assumptions, and how well the differentiable FEA matches real hardware.
paper_title: Towards joint optimization of stellarator coils and support structures
